explanation: The Sigma rule explicitly checks for the string ' administrators ' with spaces on both sides. By enclosing the group name in quotes, the process command line becomes '"Administrators"', breaking the space-padded string match required by the 'selection_group' condition.

The rule expects the explicit parameter ' -Group ' to be used in conjunction with 'Add-LocalGroupMember'. Because PowerShell supports positional parameters, omitting the explicit '-Group' flag entirely circumvents the 'selection_main' condition.

This technique utilizes the native .NET Active Directory Service Interfaces (ADSI) to manipulate local group membership directly via the API. Because it relies on neither 'net.exe' nor 'Add-LocalGroupMember', it completely bypasses the 'selection_main' condition.

The Sigma rule searches for the exact string ' administrators ' with leading and trailing spaces. By enclosing the group name in quotes, the command line lacks the trailing/leading space combinations expected by the selection_group filter, successfully evading detection.
Similar to the net command, this uses quotes around the group name to avoid matching the ' administrators ' string pattern which requires surrounding spaces.

This approach uses ADSI in PowerShell to modify the group directly via API. It bypasses the rule completely because it does not rely on net.exe or the Add-LocalGroupMember cmdlet, failing the selection_main criteria entirely.
